title: Elymus duthiei
taxonomy: Elymus duthiei is classified under the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This species belongs to the genus Elymus, which comprises a variety of grasses native to Asia and North America. Elymus duthiei is further grouped into the tribe Triticeae, a subfamily of grasses characterized by their spikelets and grass-like foliage. It is also classified under the subgenus Elymus, which includes several species of grasses native to temperate regions. Elymus duthiei is a species of grass that is closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Elymus caninus and Elymus sibiricus.

intro: Elymus duthiei, also known as Duthie's wheat grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Elymus duthiei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Elymus duthiei is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
